item.title: The Future of Sport on an Overheating Planet
item.description: What is climate change doing to sport? And — given the emissions impact of some big events — what is sport doing to climate change?

item.title: Mekong Capital Starts Climate-Focused Fund Backed by Green Group
item.description: Vietnam’s Mekong Capital Ltd. is launching a climate-focused private equity fund with a $50 million anchor investment from the Green Climate Fund.

item.title: Big Tech’s Carbon Emissions Spike With Runaway Growth of AI
item.description: Amazon and Google report jumps in their greenhouse gas output as the data center boom gains momentum.

item.title: Ivory Coast, Ghana Floods Claim 71 Lives as Rains Intensify
item.description: Heavy rains have left at least 71 people dead in Ivory Coast and Ghana over the past few days, with the worst downpour occurring Sunday and Monday.

item.title: Amazon’s Carbon Emissions Rose 16% in 2025 Amid Data Center Boom
item.description: Amazon.com Inc.’s carbon emissions rose for the second year in a row in 2025, driven by data center construction and fuel consumed by its delivery operation.

item.title: US Home Storage is Booming Even as Solar Installations Stall
item.description: The first quarter of 2026 saw record installations

item.title: New York, Chicago Grids Strain as Record Heat Wave Expands
item.description: Power grids from Chicago to New York City are bracing for sky-high demand as sweltering heat begins to build across the eastern half of the US.

item.title: Spain Saw Record Heat Deaths for June After Heat Wave Hit Europe
item.description: Spain experienced its deadliest ever June for heat mortality after record-breaking temperatures scorched Europe.

item.title: OMV CEO Says Europe Risks Energy Security on Import Restrictions
item.description: Europe is threatening its own energy security through import policies that could reduce the pool of potential gas suppliers, according to the outgoing chief executive officer of Austria’s OMV AG.

item.title: More Americans Are Installing Home Batteries. Here’s Why
item.description: The elimination of a federal tax credit drove solar installations down, but state incentives are making a difference when it comes to home energy storage.

item.title: Western Europe Braces for Repeated Heat Waves Through July
item.description: As Europe’s record-breaking heat wave recedes, the elements for the next hot spell are coalescing over the Atlantic Ocean.

item.title: Climate Change Keeps Adding to the List of Uninsurable Assets, Allianz Executive Says
item.description: Extreme weather events — such as the heat wave gripping Europe — are making a growing number of assets too risky to insure, according to a director at Europe’s largest primary insurer.

item.title: Australia’s Largest Green Hydrogen Project Secures Green Light
item.description: Orica Ltd. approved a major renewable hydrogen project, the biggest in Australia to reach the milestone.

item.title: Emergency Ordered for Largest US Power Grid as Record Heat Nears
item.description: The Trump administration has declared a power emergency for the nation’s largest energy grid in advance of a dangerous heat wave that threatens to strain electricity infrastructure.

item.title: Carney Concedes Canada Will Overshoot Emissions Forecasts
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney said Canada’s emissions will be higher in the coming years than previously projected, his clearest admission yet that the country’s near-term climate targets are likely out of reach.

item.title: Eastern US Heat Wave Set to Collide With World Cup Matches
item.description: A dangerous heat wave is expected to engulf the eastern US in the coming days, coinciding with a critical slate of World Cup football matches heading into the Fourth of July weekend.

item.title: EU Adds Palm Oil for Drugs, Soy Seeds to Deforestation Carve-Out
item.description: The EU plans to exempt palm oil derivatives used in medicines and soybeans used for sowing from its landmark rules tackling global deforestation in its latest attempt to blunt the law’s impact on sensitive sectors.

item.title: Spain’s Solar Is So Cheap Investors Are Looking for an Exit
item.description: The country has too much power on sunny days.

item.title: France’s Far Right Vows Aircon for Masses as Heat Turns Political
item.description: France’s far right is promising air conditioners now, rather than climate solutions for later, to try and sway voters as Europe bakes under record temperatures.

item.title: Air Products Cancels Massive Clean Energy Complex in Louisiana
item.description: Air Products and Chemicals Inc. said it won’t proceed with the Louisiana Clean Energy Complex, a multibillion-dollar project comprising production of hydrogen and the sequestration of carbon dioxide.

item.title: Amazon to Buy Half the Carbon Credits From Bacon Tree Project
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. said it will buy just less than half of the carbon credits to be generated by a project to restore degraded land in South Africa by planting thickets of Spekboom, a plant that sequesters carbon dioxide and helps soil retain water.

item.title: UK Grid Operator Lifts Post-2030 Investment Need to £89 Billion
item.description: The UK’s electricity transmission network needs about £89 billion ($118 billion) of investment after 2030 to cope with rising demand and more renewable energy supplies, the grid operator said.

item.title: Singapore to Begin Giant Project to Fight Rising Sea Levels
item.description: Singapore will begin a landmark coastal protection project by the end of this year, aiming to reclaim as much as 800 hectares (1,977 acres) of land to protect against rising sea levels.

item.title: South Korea to Speed Nuclear Push to Meet Surging Power Demand
item.description: The South Korean government will consider ways to cut atomic power construction times as it looks to ramp up energy supply to meet the demands of artificial intelligence.

item.title: World Bank Scraps Climate Financing Targets After US Criticism
item.description: The World Bank decided to remove quantitative targets for incorporating climate objectives into its financing, months after criticism from the US — its biggest shareholder — that it should get back to focusing on economic development.

item.title: Brookfield Sees Energy Deals Targeting Batteries Displacing Standalone Solar, Wind
item.description: Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. says contracts that pair clean energy generation with battery storage options are starting to replace pure renewables deals.

item.title: Trump Pulls Plug on Carolina Wind Project as Cancellations Mount
item.description: The Trump administration is canceling a lease held by Duke Energy Corp. off the coast of North Carolina as it expands its campaign to block new offshore wind developments.

item.title: Delhi to Ban New Gas-Powered Scooters, Trucks to Fight Pollution
item.description: India’s capital New Delhi will ban new petrol- and natural gas-fueled two-wheelers and small trucks from its roads over the next two years in one of the most aggressive moves yet by an Indian state to curb air pollution.

item.title: Paris Embraces District Cooling to Battle Deadly Heat
item.description: Europe is experiencing an early, intense heat wave.

item.title: Solar Boom Poses World Cup Test for Brazil’s Power Grid
item.description: When Brazil faces Japan in the World Cup early Monday afternoon, operators of the country’s power grid will have another concern in mind: keeping the system stable as millions of Brazilians step away from factories, offices and stores to gather in front of TVs.
